residentialUsage.message: How many hours of power cuts do you experience in a day (on average)?

noResidentialBatteryNeeded.message:
On-Grid Solar System Recommended

Since you don't experience power cuts, a On-Grid solar system without battery backup is the most cost-effective solution for you.

Benefits of On-Grid System
  • Lower Initial Cost: 30-40% less expensive than battery systems
  • Higher Efficiency: No energy loss in battery charging/discharging
  • Net Metering: Excess energy fed back to grid for credits
  • Lower Maintenance: Fewer components to maintain
  • Faster ROI: Quicker payback due to lower investment
How It Works

During the day, your solar panels generate electricity that powers your home. Any excess energy is fed back to the grid, earning you credits. At night or during low solar generation, you use grid electricity.

This system is perfect for areas with reliable grid power and will maximize your savings!

options.label: Perfect! Show me the complete estimate
Actually, I'd like to consider battery backup

options.label: Let's get quote for On-Grid system

residentialBatteryNeed.message:
Battery Backup Recommendation

Based on your power cuts of {powerCuts} hours/day, you might benefit from having battery backup with your solar system.

Why Consider Battery Backup
  • Provides power during grid outages
  • Stores excess solar energy for nighttime use
  • Increases energy independence
  • Provides backup for essential appliances

Would you be interested in including batteries in your solar system?

batteryInfo.message:
Battery Backup Information

Benefits of Battery Backup
  • Energy Independence: Reduced reliance on the grid
  • Power Security: Electricity during outages
  • Maximize Solar Usage: Store excess daytime energy for night use
  • Stabilize Power: Protection from voltage fluctuations
Important Considerations
  • Cost Impact: Adds approximately 30-40% to system cost
  • Battery Lifespan: Modern lithium batteries last 8-10 years
  • Maintenance: Minimal maintenance required
  • Space Requirements: Requires dedicated installation area
Return on Investment

While batteries increase initial cost, they can be economically advantageous in areas with:

  • Frequent or long power outages
  • Time-of-use electricity pricing
  • High grid electricity rates

options.label: Yes, include battery in my estimate

Your Complete Hybrid Solar Solution

Battery backup cost:Rs. {batteryCost}
Total system cost:Rs. {totalSystemCost}
PM Surya Ghar subsidy:Rs. {subsidyAmount}

Hybrid System Advantages
  • Energy Independence: Power during grid outages
  • Maximum Solar Utilization: Store excess energy for later use
  • Net Metering Benefits: Still connected to grid for credits
  • Load Management: Automatic switching between solar, battery, and grid
  • Backup Security: Essential appliances powered during outages
Battery Performance
  • Backup duration: {backupHours} hours for essential loads
  • Daily cycles: Optimized for daily charge/discharge
  • Warranty: 8-10 years or 6000+ cycles
  • Efficiency: 95%+ round-trip efficiency
Lifetime Benefits
  • Annual energy generation: {energyGeneration} units (kWh)

  • Total 25-year savings: Rs. {lifetimeSavings}
  • Carbon footprint reduction: ~{carbonOffset} tons CO₂/year

Solar Assessment

Great! Let's find the perfect solar solution for you.

What type of property are you looking to install solar panels on?

pricingInfoFlow.message:
Solar Pricing Information

Solar panel pricing depends on several factors. Here's a general overview:

Residential Solar Pricing (2024)
  • 1kW System: Rs. 74,500 - Rs. 85,000
  • 3kW System: Rs. 2,25,000 - Rs. 2,55,000
  • 5kW System: Rs. 3,50,000 - Rs. 4,00,000
Government Subsidies Available
  • 1kW: Rs. 30,000 subsidy
  • 2kW: Rs. 60,000 subsidy
  • 3kW: Rs. 78,000 subsidy
Factors Affecting Price
  • System size and capacity
  • Panel quality and technology
  • Installation complexity
  • Location and accessibility
  • Battery backup requirements

Would you like a personalized quote based on your specific needs?
